name: Smartphones and mobile learning for teachers and rural learners
description: Interventions that introduce smartphones and mobile devices into teacher support programs, classrooms, and self-directed learning in low-resource and rural contexts. Spans ethnographic studies of smartphone usage by emergent users, feasibility studies in schools, and impact evaluations of mobile learning adoption.

name: AI/ML mapping of educational access and vulnerability
description: Use of satellite imagery, weakly-supervised deep learning, and open frameworks to map schools, 'education deserts,' and disadvantaged populations for connectivity and resource targeting. These projects treat spatial data science as a tool for identifying where educational access is lacking.

name: AI tutoring and child-computer interaction for learning
description: Systems that use AI—intelligent tutoring systems, GenAI, sign-language interpretation, and child-computer interaction tools—to deliver or personalize instruction, often in the Global South. Includes concerns about equity, fairness, and support for marginalized learners.

name: Digital and data literacy measurement for low-experience populations
description: Research on defining, building, and validating digital/data literacy for populations with limited internet experience. Focuses on measurement instruments and conceptual frameworks that underpin literacy interventions rather than a specific deployed tool.

name: One-device-per-learner hardware initiatives
description: Programs premised on distributing purpose-built or repurposed computing hardware directly to children and schools, such as OLPC and landfill-computer reuse. These share a design philosophy of empowerment through device ownership and confront sustainability and infrastructure realities.

name: Teacher-centered technology support and collaboration
description: Interventions and studies focused on teachers as the locus of change—supporting teacher collaboration, aspirations and goal-setting, and understanding factors affecting classroom ICT adoption. Distinct from student-facing tools by treating educators' practices and constraints as the design target.

name: Women's empowerment through educational and vocational technology
description: Projects that use smartphones, vocational training (CVET), participatory media, and tailored apps to empower rural and village women through education and skill-building. Explicitly foregrounds gender as the organizing axis of the intervention.

name: System-scale education reform and equity in the Global South
description: Large-scale efforts to fix or scale national/regional education systems, including Teaching at the Right Level, out-of-school girls' enrollment, and rural-urban equity programs. Emphasizes operations, scale, and structural equity rather than single-classroom tools.

name: ICT integration in resource-constrained schools
description: Studies of the enabling and constraining conditions for deploying ICT infrastructure in under-resourced schools, covering connectivity, cybersecurity, curriculum alignment, and disaster resilience. These examine institutional and infrastructural readiness as much as the technology itself.
